source.title: Dynamic models of large-scale brain activity

confidence_rationale: Canonical review articulating metastability as the core operating principle of large-scale brain dynamics: the brain transiently dwells in attractor-like states and transitions between them with characteristic dwell-time distributions. Directly supports MECH-047's claim of mode-commitment hysteresis. Mapping from whole-brain attractor dynamics to REE's three cognitive modes is structurally faithful. Main transfer risks are from network-level dynamics to the discrete mode-manager abstraction and from resting-state to task-engaged circuits.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The brain operates near a multistable regime where neural activity transiently visits quasi-stable attractor states (metastability). Transitions between states are not instantaneous — the system exhibits dwell times within each state, and crossing to a new attractor requires crossing an energy barrier. This yields history-dependent (hysteretic) dynamics at the network level.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-047: The REE control plane mode manager implements cognitive-mode hysteresis by analogy to neural metastability. Once a mode (doing, ready-vigilance, default-mode) is entered, the system dwells in that attractor basin and resists switching until arousal input (modelled as LC-NE; see TRK-LC) supplies sufficient energy to cross the inter-state barrier. The switching cost is the REE model of the energy-barrier cost documented in Breakspear's dynamic models.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Breakspear's review addresses whole-brain large-scale networks in resting and lightly task-engaged states; the three REE modes do not map one-to-one to the attractor states characterised in the review. The review treats metastability as an emergent network property; in MECH-047 hysteresis is implemented as an explicit control-plane gate, which is a stronger mechanistic claim than the review supports. DOI and citation details are verified; mapping caveat is interpretive, not bibliographic.
mapping.source_context: Review of computational and biophysical models of large-scale brain dynamics, including Stuart-Landau oscillators, multi-stable mean-field models, and empirical resting-state fMRI dwell-time statistics. Covers both local (cortical column) and whole-brain (connectome-constrained) levels.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality canonical review; mapping fidelity is good but not tight because REE's discrete three-mode abstraction is more coarse-grained than the continuous attractor landscapes discussed. Transfer risk reflects the resting-state vs. goal-directed task context and the network-to-control-plane abstraction gap.
